prompt:
An analog compost moisture meter with a long 20 cm stainless steel probe is shown partially inserted into a dark, crumbly compost pile inside a bin or tumbler. The round dial at the top is angled toward the viewer, its needle pointing to a mid-range zone, but the numbers and labels are too small to read, suggesting a 0–100% scale without exposing exact markings. Bits of organic matter cling lightly to the probe, clearly indicating it is designed to gauge compost dampness at depth.

The surrounding environment is a near-future solarpunk garden space with raised beds, lush plants, and soft outdoor lighting. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable text on the meter, and a composition that highlights the probe, dial, and compost surface while the rest of the garden and tools remain softly blurred, emphasizing practical, data-informed compost care.
